creator: jeffrey_in_nyc
caption: How to spend a day in Manhattan for under $20 #nyc #NewYork #travel #food #nybucketlist

transcription.text: Here is how to spend a full day in Manhattan for under $20. You'll start your day at Hudson Yards to grab a coffee and a croissant from Blue Bottle and then head outside to capture views of the vessel. This hooks right onto the High Line, a 1.5 mile elevated walking path that runs along an old rail line and down the Hudson River, offering breathtaking views of the city. Once you hit 14th Street, you'll head over to Pier 57, go inside, pass the market, until you find this elevator. Hit the button that says park, then you'll step out onto this beautiful free outdoor rooftop with sweeping views of the city. After this, we'll head east and into the Meatpacking District to see the cobblestone streets and historic buildings. Take just a few steps south and you'll be in the West Village, grabbing a slice at Lindustri, one of the best pizza shops in all of New York. Then we'll walk over to Greenwich Street, southbound, because it's more relaxed, quiet, and less touristy with stunning townhome architecture. We'll take this all the way down through Hudson Square, see the beautiful streets of Trebeka and the Woolworth Building, and then eventually end up at World Trade Center. Follow for more New York City tours.
